message:
fix(pager): clear writes_observed on rollback paths (#70 follow-up)

The ghost-commit defensive guard introduced in b37f9053 sets `writes_
observed = true` whenever `write_page` or `write_page_data` stages a
page. The guard correctness requires clearing this bit whenever the
transaction's staged writes are legitimately discarded — otherwise a
subsequent empty-write_set commit that follows a valid rollback (e.g.
BEGIN; INSERT; ROLLBACK TO sp; COMMIT with an empty snapshot) would
wrongly trip the guard.

Two cleanup sites:

- `SimpleTransaction::rollback`: unconditionally clear `writes_observed`
  — a full rollback drops every staged write.

- `SimpleTransaction::rollback_to_savepoint`: clear only when the
  savepoint snapshot's write_set is empty, so the restored state
  accurately tracks whether any pending writes remain. If the snapshot
  had entries the restored write_set still contains them and the guard
  continues to track correctly.

Self-connection tests + peer-races still pass. Pre-existing
`test_failed_implicit_savepoint_does_not_leave_transaction_open`
failure is unchanged (unrelated to the guard — the test also fails on
main without this change).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
